Um The last thing I wanna mention is that all of our data is 100% publicly available data.

A zero would be the worst.
But whenever we come up with these scores, we look at things like location.

The other property has been built or renovated asset class.

So the reason we call this advertised occupancy versus just regular occupancy is because it's based off the known number of vacant units in the property.

And what I mean by that is sometimes properties will hide availability if they're trying to serve demand or or not look like they have, you know, all this excess inventory, they might, you know, for example, they might have 31 bedrooms available.
However, they only may decide to make 10 of those available to the property website.
Our system can only account for the 10 that are made available.
So this goes back to the private versus public data publicly.
That's all we know that exists privately.
